Could we soften this clause a little?

Once we both sign, the agreement is binding.
I am glad we are here. Before I wire anything, my team runs due diligence. I will need audited accounts and proof of compliance with the export rules. Nothing personal, it is just how we work.
That is fine. I will disclose everything: two years of audited accounts and all the supplier contracts. As long as your team has clear questions, we will answer them fast.
Good. There is one clause I care about: you do not take a competing backer. I will not close unless that is in the agreement.
I understand it, but as written it is open-ended. We can agree, provided that the non-compete is limited to one year. After that, I need to be free.
One year is fair. And I will waive the early-exit penalty on condition that you hit the first milestone. Once we both sign, it is binding, for both of us.
Then we have a deal. We undertake to keep the records open for an annual audit, and we will sign as soon as due diligence is done. Thank you, Diana, for pushing on the terms.
We will move to signing as long as due diligence is clean.
We accept the clause, provided that the non-compete is one year.
We will not close unless the audit passes.

Hi Milton, it's Diana. Good news first: I'm in. I loved the pitch, and the numbers hold up, so we are moving to closing. Before I wire anything, my team needs to run due diligence, which is normal, please don't read anything into it. I'll send a list, but the main things are simple. I need to see audited accounts for the last two years, your supplier contracts, and confirmation that you're fully compliant with export rules. As long as those check out, we can sign quickly. One condition from my side: the agreement will include a clause that you won't take on a competing backer for the first year. That's standard, and I'm happy to explain it. Unless something strange turns up, I'd like to have a binding agreement signed within three weeks. Send me the documents whenever you're ready, and call me if any term feels unfair. I'd rather fix it now than after we sign. Talk soon.

Hey, it's Milton, just a quick note to myself the day we signed. A year ago I could barely get through a phone call in English, and today I read every clause of an investment agreement, negotiated two of them, and signed. Diana's team ran due diligence, they wanted audited accounts and proof of compliance, and because everything was clean, it went fast. I didn't just nod along this time. When one clause felt too tight, I asked her to change it, and I said we could agree provided that the non-compete was limited to one year. She said yes. I undertook to keep the records open for an annual audit, and she agreed to waive the early penalty as long as we hit the first milestone. The deal is binding now, and the strange thing is I'm not nervous. I understood every word I signed. That is the difference a year makes. The money matters, but reading that contract and holding my own in the negotiation, that is the real win.
